heading: Forgery or false use of passport
label: 1543 Forgery or false use of passport
text: Whoever falsely makes, forges, counterfeits, mutilates, or alters any passport or instrument purporting to be a passport, with intent that the same may be used; orWhoever willfully and knowingly uses, or attempts to use, or furnishes to another for use any such false, forged, counterfeited, mutilated, or altered passport or instrument purporting to be a passport, or any passport validly issued which has become void by the occurrence of any condition therein prescribed invalidating the same—Shall be fined under this title, imprisoned not more than 25 years (if the offense was committed to facilitate an act of international terrorism (as defined in )), 20 years (if the offense was committed to facilitate a drug trafficking crime (as defined in )), 10 years (in the case of the first or second such offense, if the offense was not committed to facilitate such an act of international terrorism or a drug trafficking crime), or 15 years (in the case of any other offense), or both.section 2331 of this titlesection 929(a) of this title
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Foreign Relations and Intercourse (, title IX, § 4, ; , ).section 222 of title 22June 15, 1917, ch. 3040 Stat. 227Mar. 28, 1940, ch. 72, § 754 Stat. 80Reference to persons causing or procuring was omitted as unnecessary in view of definition of “principal” in .section 2 of this titleMandatory-punishment provision with authorization for added fine in discretion of court was rephrased in the alternative.Punishment of five years’ imprisonment was substituted for “ten years” to conform with other sections embracing offenses of comparable gravity.Minor changes were made in phraseology.

Amendments2002— substituted “to facilitate” for “to facility” in last par.Pub. L. 107–2731996— substituted “imprisoned not more than 25 years (if the offense was committed to facilitate an act of international terrorism (as defined in )), 20 years (if the offense was committed to facilitate a drug trafficking crime (as defined in )), 10 years (in the case of the first or second such offense, if the offense was not committed to facility such an act of international terrorism or a drug trafficking crime), or 15 years (in the case of any other offense)” for “imprisoned not more than 10 years” in last par.Pub. L. 104–208section 2331 of this titlesection 929(a) of this title1994—, which directed the amendment of this section by substituting “under this title” for “not more than $2,000”, could not be executed because the words “not more than $2,000” did not appear in text subsequent to amendment by . See below.Pub. L. 103–322, § 330016(1)(I)Pub. L. 103–322, § 130009(a)(2), substituted “under this title, imprisoned not more than 10 years” for “not more than $2,000 or imprisoned not more than five years” in last par.Pub. L. 103–322, § 130009(a)(2)

Effective Date of 1996 AmendmentAmendment by applicable with respect to offenses occurring on or after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 104–208section 211(c) of Pub. L. 104–208section 1028 of this titleSept. 30, 1996
